description: Repo-local dogfood receipts for real Lane 1 repair-loop attempts, including successful, resolved, unchanged, and missing-receipt outcomes.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: readiness and attempt-ledger report assertions over repair_kind attempted/improved/unchanged/regressed counts
cases.attempted_repair: Added repair-route quality rollups and top failing/missing evidence route fields to attempt-ledger/readiness outputs.
cases.evidence_movement: Readiness can now show repair_kind success-rate evidence instead of only packet counts.
cases.operator_note: The route-quality attempt improved analyzer-backlog signal without changing badge, LSP, PR, or CI authority.
cases.reason: A real merged repair-loop PR converted attempt outcomes into route-quality evidence.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: readiness JSON and Markdown assertions that top_next_action equals next_actions[0]
cases.attempted_repair: Added a stable top_next_action projection for downstream surfaces to consume directly.
cases.evidence_movement: The missing readiness projection gap was resolved by preserving next_actions[0] as a named contract field.
cases.operator_note: This makes LSP/PR/CI/badge projection thinner because they do not need to reinterpret raw findings for the first useful action.
cases.reason: A real merged readiness PR closed the missing top-next-action projection gap.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: dogfood fixture proving one receipt-backed packet preserves canonical identity through attempt-ledger and readiness
cases.attempted_repair: Added a surface-projection alignment dogfood corpus and dogfood report section.
cases.evidence_movement: The packet identity, repair kind, verify command, receipt command/state, outcome, and readiness top_next_action are now pinned together.
cases.operator_note: This improved proof that downstream surfaces are thin consumers of canonical state, but it deliberately did not change those surfaces.
cases.reason: A real merged dogfood PR added receipt-backed projection alignment proof.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: targeted-test outcome fixture with unchanged before/after grip evidence
cases.attempted_repair: Exercised the outcome join where targeted movement exists but the static evidence did not improve.
cases.evidence_movement: Outcome reporting kept the unchanged attempt visible and did not mark the gap resolved.
cases.operator_note: This is intentionally not a success case; it proves failed or insufficient attempts do not disappear.
cases.reason: A real dogfood outcome pass recorded unchanged evidence as visible non-success.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: report golden observer for rendered configuration label
cases.attempted_repair: Dry-ran a ready packet and checked verify/receipt command separation without fabricating missing receipts.
cases.evidence_movement: The verify command passed, while the receipt command named required missing receipt files.
cases.operator_note: This records a non-closed loop explicitly; a missing receipt remains a blocker rather than hidden success.
cases.missing_receipt_reason: receipt command reported required local receipts were absent
cases.reason: A real dogfood dry-run pass proved missing receipts stay named and do not become implicit success.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: fixture assertions that local/computed boundary operands remain named static limitations and do not emit add_boundary_assertion packets
cases.attempted_repair: Pinned local/computed boundary operand cases in evidence-quality and swarm-plan corpora so the analyzer backlog route stays precise and non-actionable.
cases.evidence_movement: The local/computed activation boundary route now has fixture-backed proof that RIPR names the limitation instead of inventing candidate values or public repair packets.
cases.operator_note: This is a successful negative-capability repair-loop attempt: it improved route trust by making unsafe actionability stay blocked.
must_not_change: public projection eligibility for static limitations
cases.reason: A real merged limitation-route PR made local/computed boundary operands more trustworthy by preserving named non-actionability instead of routing unsafe repair work.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: evidence-record and benchmark fixture assertions that assertion-target affinity remains a named static limitation and does not satisfy activation
cases.attempted_repair: Split assertion-target affinity owner-call absence into a route-specific static limitation while preserving non-actionable status.
cases.evidence_movement: The assertion-target affinity route now has fixture-backed proof that RIPR names the analyzer limitation instead of treating token affinity as activation evidence.
cases.operator_note: This is a successful negative-capability repair-loop attempt: it improved analyzer backlog precision without emitting a public repair packet.
must_not_change: public projection eligibility for static limitations
cases.reason: A real merged limitation-route PR made assertion-target affinity more trustworthy by routing it to a precise analyzer backlog item while keeping it non-actionable.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: evidence-record and swarm-plan fixture assertions that iterator-derived boundary operands remain named static limitations instead of public repair packets
cases.attempted_repair: Routed iterator-derived activation-boundary operands to a precise static-limitation analyzer backlog instead of speculative boundary assertion guidance.
cases.evidence_movement: Iterator-derived operands now have fixture-backed proof that RIPR refuses to invent activation values and names the analyzer route required to unlock actionability.
cases.operator_note: This is a negative-capability repair-loop attempt: the useful outcome is sharper non-actionability, not a public packet.
must_not_change: public projection eligibility for static limitations
synthetic activation values for iterator-derived operands
repair_kind add_boundary_assertion for unresolved iterator operands
cases.reason: A real merged analyzer PR made iterator-derived boundary operands more trustworthy by routing them to a precise limitation route while keeping them non-actionable.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: serve_stdio call-presence observer asserting the LSP stdio entrypoint still constructs and serves the tower LSP server
cases.receipt_command: ripr agent receipt --root . --verify-json target/ripr/workflow/agent-verify.json --seam-id c4abcb8dc04cf9c4 --json --out target/ripr/reports/agent-receipt.json
cases.attempted_repair: Added serve_stdio_call_presence_observer in the packet's allowed edit surface, crates/ripr/src/lsp/tests.rs.
cases.evidence_movement: The narrow observer test passed, but the formal after repo-exposure snapshot generation timed out before agent verify or receipt could complete.
cases.operator_note: This is an honest failed handoff proof: the packet was bounded enough to attempt, but its generated verify route was too broad to receipt in this run.
cases.missing_receipt_reason: formal agent verify receipt was not emitted because generating target/ripr/pilot/after.repo-exposure.json timed out after 20 minutes
cases.reason: A real queued packet attempt exposed that the default repo-exposure verify route can be too broad for a bounded test-only repair handoff.
